title: Evangelion 20 in Hong Kong
wikitext:
[]: Evangelion 2.0 in Hong Kong             Quote Post by Electric Sachiel » Thu Dec 03, 2009 2:45 am Damn it all. Just my luck. But recently i got word that Evangelion 2.0 opened in Hong Kong just a few hours ago. It was scheduled to open in the city on December 3rd 2009. Its scheduled to continue playing in Hong Kong across three different theater locations for the next few days. Possibly extending to a week. Not sure how long it'll continue playing. I know a friend who lives there and many other relatives. But darn it most of them dont even seem to like Eva that much. LOL. I hate that feeling that you get when you miss out on all those cool and awesome events just because you dont live close by. I'm like a continent away be...jesus! Anyways for those of you who may be lucky enough to live in Hong Kong heres the facebook link to the movie page for chinese readers. [url]http://www.facebook.com/group.php?gid=96429694265[/url] And heres the showtimes for each theater location for those lucky bastards out there. [url]http://www.mclcinema.com/visMovieSelect.aspx?visLang=2[/url] Jesus. Multiple showtimes that play the movie several times a day.... Even funimation didnt give us that many options when Eva 1.0 was released here a few months back. Gah.....why oh why!!?? Well i hope this helps you Eva fans in HK. Lucky some of a *****!!! ;)

[]:             Quote Post by darabo » Sat Dec 05, 2009 12:46 pm Do you or anyone else know if the movie has English subtitles? Because I would love to go see this in the cinema if there are English subs.

[]:             Quote Post by Electric Sachiel » Sat Dec 05, 2009 6:18 pm darabo wrote: Do you or anyone else know if the movie has English subtitles? Because I would love to go see this in the cinema if there are English subs.

[]:             Quote Post by Fazmotron » Sat Dec 05, 2009 8:08 pm I would see it regardless of subtitles.

[]:             Quote Post by wonderluster » Sat Dec 05, 2009 8:10 pm Same here, raw or subs, if it's good quality and its nearby , Im there.

[]:             Quote Post by darabo » Thu Dec 10, 2009 12:31 pm Thanks everyone, I saw it yesterday and I have to say it is awesome. Much better than the first Rebuild movie. Also I got a book which looks like it has pictures from the movie and I think there is an interview along with some concept art. It kind of sucks that most of it is in Chinese. If anyone wants to see it than I would be more than happy to take pictures of the booklet.

[]:             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Thu Dec 10, 2009 1:24 pm darabo wrote: If anyone wants to see it than I would be more than happy to take pictures of the booklet.
